body: Version: Deno 2.9.2 I cannot for the life of me find a way to configure `cef` when using it for `deno desktop`, which is holding me up as I try to migrate my three.js app from electron to deno When i navigate the cef window to `chrome://gpu` I get this: For posterity, this is the same window in Chrome proper: Can we either add some documentation explaining how to configure cef, or add a way to specify flags for cef? I couldn't figure out how to do it if its possible, and I'd potentially be willing to contribute, I just need some guidance on what this should look like for the cli/deno.json.

title: node:crypto: raw `chacha20` cipher missing from createCipheriv (only the AEAD `chacha20-poly1305` is supported)
author: mattiasrunge
body:
Version: Deno 2.9.2

Node.js supports two ChaCha20 ciphers in `node:crypto`: the raw stream cipher `chacha20` and the AEAD `chacha20-poly1305`. Deno implemented only the AEAD (in 05688d17f7b4804c27b81c7762c5b451079fa3cd, closing #28411). `createCipheriv("chacha20", ...)` throws `Unknown cipher`, and `chacha20` is absent from `crypto.getCiphers()`, so code that runs on Node fails on Deno.

## Reproduction

```js
// repro.mjs
import crypto from "node:crypto";

console.log(crypto.getCiphers().filter((c) => c.includes("chacha")));

const key = Buffer.alloc(32, 1);
const iv = Buffer.alloc(16, 2); // OpenSSL layout: 32-bit LE counter || 96-bit nonce
const cipher = crypto.createCipheriv("chacha20", key, iv);
console.log(cipher.update(Buffer.alloc(8)).toString("hex"));
```

Node v24.15.0:

```
[ 'chacha20', 'chacha20-poly1305' ]
10e44b1c6e11f265
```

Deno 2.9.2 (`deno run repro.mjs`):

```
[ "chacha20-poly1305" ]
error: Unknown cipher
```

## Expected behavior

`createCipheriv`/`createDecipheriv` accept `"chacha20"` with a 32-byte key and a 16-byte IV (interpreted as 32-bit little-endian initial block counter followed by a 96-bit nonce, matching OpenSSL's EVP chacha20), and `"chacha20"` is listed by `crypto.getCiphers()`.

## Why this matters

The AEAD is not a substitute for the raw cipher. One concrete case: the SSH transport cipher `chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com` requires ChaCha20 keystream with explicit control of the block counter (including counter 0, which the AEAD reserves internally for Poly1305 key derivation) and a detached MAC over a custom input. Libraries implementing SSH/other protocols that use Node's `chacha20` today must fall back to userland implementations on Deno.

The building block already exists in the codebase: the `chacha20` Rust crate was added as a dependency for the AEAD implementation in `ext/node_crypto/cipher.rs`, so this is mostly a matter of wiring up the non-AEAD variant.

title: Long package file names trigger "Provided value is too long when setting path" in deno pack
author: RealityAnomaly
body:
Version: Deno 2.9.2

When using `deno pack` on a project with long file names such as `src/kubernetes/models/_schemas/IoK8sApiAdmissionregistrationV1beta1ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList.d.ts`, `deno pack` exits with an error:

```
  1325 assets collected
error: provided value is too long when setting path for package/src/kubernetes/apimachinery/_schemas/
```

title: spurious `links` warning in linked packages
author: NeaByteLab
body:
Deno warns that a `links` field is illegal in a non-root package, but then still
uses that same `links` field to resolve dependencies. Both can't be true.

## Version

```
deno 2.9.2 (also 2.9.0)
```

## Project structure

```
deno-repro/
├── core/
│   ├── deno.json         name @repro/core
│   └── src/index.ts      exports core()
├── middle/
│   ├── deno.json         name @repro/middle, links ["../core"]
│   └── src/index.ts      imports @repro/core
└── top/
    ├── deno.json         name @repro/top, links ["../core", "../middle"]
    └── src/index.ts      imports @repro/middle
```

Dependency direction: `top` imports `middle`, `middle` imports `core`.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A["cd top and run: deno check src/"] --> B["top is the active root"]
  B --> C["Deno reads middle as a linked package"]
  C --> D{"middle declares a links field"}
  D -->|treated as non-root config| W["Warning: links can only be specified in workspace root"]
  D -->|but the field is still used| E["resolve @repro/core through middle links"]
  E --> F["fetch core/src/index.ts, success"]
  F --> G["type check passes, exit 0"]

  classDef warn fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,color:#000;
  classDef ok fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,color:#000;
  class W warn;
  class G ok;
```

Both outcomes come from the same `links` field in `middle`: it is flagged as
illegal (W) yet used successfully to resolve `core` (G).

## Reproduce

```sh
cd top
deno check src/
```

## Expected

No warning. `top` is the active root and already links `../core` and
`../middle`, so resolution succeeds.

## Actual

Type check succeeds (exit 0), but the warning is emitted twice:

```
Warning "links" field can only be specified in the workspace root deno.json file.
    at .../middle/deno.json
```

Checking `middle` on its own (`cd middle && deno check src/`) emits no warning,
because `middle` is then the active root.

[deno-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29950646/deno-repro.zip)

title: H.264/MP4 codec support in CEF backend?
author: yuchiXiong
body:
# H.264/MP4 codec support in CEF backend?

  ## Summary

  I'm exploring Deno Desktop with the CEF backend and noticed that MP4 videos with H.264 codec don't play. I understand this is a common limitation with CEF builds due to proprietary codec licensing.

  ## Question

  Is there a plan to provide H.264/AAC codec support for the CEF backend, or is this something users need to handle themselves by building custom CEF binaries?

  ## Context

  ```typescript
  Deno.serve(() =>
    new Response("<video width='300px' controls autoplay src='https://example.com/video.mp4'></video>", {
      headers: { "content-type": "text/html" },
    })
  );
  ```

  When building with `deno desktop build --backend cef main.ts`, the video element fails to play MP4 files.

  Background

  CEF typically excludes proprietary codecs like H.264 and AAC from default builds due to licensing requirements. To enable these codecs, CEF needs to be built with `proprietary_codecs=true` and `ffmpeg_branding=Chrome` flags.

  I'd love to understand the team's perspective on this. Thanks!

  Environment

  - Deno version: 2.9+
  - Platform: macOS
  - Backend: --backend cef

title: deno update panics on on invalid minimum-dependency-age
author: grqz
body:
Version: Deno 2.9.2

There are two types of bugs, one is `DateTime - TimeDelta` overflow, the other is TimeDelta::minutes out of bounds (when `ceil(INT64_MAX/60'000) < #min <= INT64_MAX`).
138939752217 is the number of minutes that have elapsed since `-262143-01-01 00:00:00 UTC` (rounded down). You might need to increase it to reproduce the error.
```console
$ export RUST_BACKTRACE=full
$ deno update --minimum-dependency-age=138939752217
All dependencies are up to date.
$ deno update --minimum-dependency-age=138939752218

Deno has panicked. This is a bug in Deno. Please report this
at https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/new.
If you can reliably reproduce this panic, include the
reproduction steps and re-run with the RUST_BACKTRACE=1 env
var set and include the backtrace in your report.
```

title: BUG: deno compile on Windows: child_process.spawn with extra stdio pipe fds fails intermittently with "The handle is invalid. (os error 6)"
author: izelnakri
body:
### Summary

In a **`deno compile`d executable on Windows**, `node:child_process.spawn()` intermittently fails with `Error: The handle is invalid. (os error 6)` when the child is spawned with **extra stdio pipe file descriptors** (fds 3/4) — the configuration Playwright uses to launch Firefox/WebKit via its "pipe" browser-protocol transport.

The failure is **intermittent** (most launches succeed) and surfaces as an **uncaught promise rejection**, so it cannot be caught around the `spawn`/`launch` call — it crashes the whole process. Chromium is unaffected because it's driven over a TCP/CDP socket with only standard stdio (fds 0–2).

I hit this reliably in CI running a `deno compile`d CLI that uses `playwright-core` to launch Firefox on `windows-latest`. It does **not** happen with the Node build of the same program (Node's `child_process` handles the extra pipe fds fine), and does **not** happen for Chromium.

### Environment

- Deno: `v2.x` via `denoland/setup-deno@v2` (latest 2.x as of 2026-07-12; also have 2.8.0 stable locally)
- OS: `windows-latest` (GitHub Actions runner)
- Binary produced by `deno compile`; failure in the `node:child_process` compat layer

### Actual stack (from Playwright `firefox.launch()` inside the compiled binary)

```
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: The handle is invalid. (os error 6)
  const spawnedProcess = childProcess.spawn(options.command, options.args || [], spawnOptions);
                                      ^
    at nodeSpawnChild (ext:deno_process/40_process.js)
    at ChildProcess.#spawnInternal (ext:deno_node/internal/child_process.ts)
    at ChildProcess.spawn (ext:deno_node/internal/child_process.ts)
    at Object.spawn (ext:deno_node/child_process.ts)
    at launchProcess (.../playwright-core/lib/server/utils/processLauncher.js:118)
    at Firefox._launchProcess (.../playwright-core/lib/server/browserType.js:205)
    at async Firefox._innerLaunch (.../playwright-core/lib/server/browserType.js:112)
    at async Firefox._innerLaunchWithRetries (.../playwright-core/lib/server/browserType.js:99)
```

### Candidate minimal reproduction

> ⚠️ I developed this reduced case on Linux and have **not** yet run it on Windows — the definitive reproduction I've observed is the Playwright launch above. Sharing this so someone on Windows can confirm the isolated trigger (extra stdio pipe fds).

```ts
// repro.ts
import { spawn } from "node:child_process";
// fds 3 & 4 are the extra pipes Playwright uses for Firefox/WebKit ("pipe" transport).
const child = spawn("cmd.exe", ["/c", "exit 0"], {
  stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe", "pipe", "pipe"],
});
child.on("error", (e) => { console.error("ERROR:", e); Deno.exit(1); });
child.on("spawn", () => console.log("spawned OK"));
child.on("exit", (c) => console.log("exit", c));
```

```
deno compile -A -o repro.exe repro.ts
.\repro.exe            # observed inside a real app: "The handle is invalid. (os error 6)"
deno run  -A repro.ts  # comparison — expected to print "spawned OK" / "exit 0"
```

### Expected

The compiled binary spawns the child (with extra pipe fds) identically to `deno run` / Node, emitting `spawn` / `exit`.

### Actual

`os error 6` (`ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE`) from `nodeSpawnChild`, surfacing as an uncaught rejection.

### Notes

- Standard 3-fd spawns work in the same compiled binary on Windows (I spawn Chrome that way successfully); only the extra-pipe-fd case fails.
- Real-world impact: Playwright `firefox.launch()` / `webkit.launch()` cannot run inside a `deno compile` binary on Windows.

title: desktop: raw backend panics on window resize
author: Giesch
body:
Version: Deno 2.9.2

The width and height setters on `UnsafeWindowSurface` cause a panic by holding the borrow guard on `SurfaceData` while calling `context.resize()`. I ran into this on Wayland, but I suspect it's not platform-specific.

To reproduce, run [the example here](https://docs.deno.com/runtime/desktop/webgpu/#a-minimal-example) with the `raw` backend. Removing the `surface.width` and `surface.height` lines prevents the panic.

The change looks small, so I plan to open a PR.

title: deno x ignores --minimum-dependency-age and keeps using the effective release-age policy
author: mommysgoodpuppy
body:
`deno x` does not appear to pass `--minimum-dependency-age` to package
resolution. As a result, users cannot opt out of the release-age policy for a
freshly published one-off CLI, nor choose a stricter age.

This affects both JSR targets and Deno's npx-style unprefixed npm targets (for
example, `deno x create-vite`).

### Reproduction

From an empty directory, use a package with an executable whose current version
is newer than the configured age window:

```sh
# Deno 2.9.2
deno x -A --minimum-dependency-age=0 jsr:@scope/cli --help
deno x -A --minimum-dependency-age=10080 jsr:@scope/cli --help
```

The same applies to an unprefixed npm target:

```sh
deno x -A --minimum-dependency-age=0 some-npm-cli --help
```

### Actual behavior

Both commands use the pre-existing effective policy or the 24h default, not the CLI value. In my environment, 
`.npmrc` sets `min-release-age=3`, and both commands report
a cutoff three days earlier. The `0` command fails with:

```txt
error: Could not find version of '@scope/cli' that matches specified version constraint '*'

A newer matching version was found, but it was not used because it was newer than the specified minimum dependency date of <three-day cutoff>
```

`minimumDependencyAge: 0` in an auto-discovered `deno.json` is ignored as
well. By comparison, `deno run --minimum-dependency-age=0` resolves the same
recent JSR package.

### Expected behavior

The explicit CLI flag and `deno.json` setting should override `.npmrc` (or
Deno's default):

- `0` disables the restriction.
- `10080` permits only versions at least seven days old.

`deno x --help` documents `0` as disabling the restriction.

### Environment

```txt
deno 2.9.2 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)
v8 14.9.207.2-rusty
typescript 6.0.3
```

title: [fount auto-report] Deno panic: assertion failed: self.is_closed.compare_exchange(false, true, Ordering::Relaxed, Ordering::Relaxed).is_ok() (2.9.2+786457c)
author: steve02081504
body:
This issue was auto-generated by [`fount test`](https://github.com/steve02081504/fount/commit/d25d5ec4bab57e96bba4cbf7d0611f541a4066bb) after it detected a Deno panic during a test run. If it duplicates an existing report, please close it. If you are being spammed by these auto-reports, that means `fount test` has a panic-deduplication bug — please report it at https://github.com/steve02081504/fount/issues.

- **Deno version**: `2.9.2+786457c`

title: allow `"https://esm.sh/": "npm:",` in import map.
author: steve02081504
body: for now it's just `Import map: Invalid target address "npm:" for package specifier "https://esm.sh/". Package address targets must end with "/".`

title: Migrate Deno lib names off the `lib` compiler option (lib.deno / lib.deno.worker)
author: bartlomieju
body:
## Problem

Deno's forked TypeScript ships ~15 granular libs selectable via the `lib`
compiler option (`deno.window`, `deno.ns`, `deno.worker`, `deno.unstable`,
`deno.shared_globals`, `deno.web`, `deno.fetch`, `deno.net`, …). Stock/native
TypeScript's `lib` option only accepts its hardcoded list and has **no way to
register custom lib names**, so any `lib: ["deno.window"]` (etc.) errors under
native tsc:

```
TS6046: Argument for '--lib' option must be: 'es5', … 'esnext', 'dom', …
```

Consolidation alone doesn't fix this — the Deno globals have to leave the `lib`
option entirely.

## Proposal

- Collapse the granular fork libs into two bundles: **`lib.deno`** (main/window
  scope) and **`lib.deno.worker`** (worker scope), keeping `deno.unstable` as an
  opt-in.
- Deliver them the stock-tsc way — via `types` / `@types/deno` / typeRoots (which
  `deno sync-types` already generates) and/or `/// <reference types="deno" />` —
  **not** via the `lib` compiler option. A Deno project selects globals with
  `types: ["deno"]` (or auto-load), never `lib: ["deno.window"]`.

## Migration / compat

- `deno sync-types` strips a user's `lib: ["deno.*"]`, ensures the matching deno
  types package is loaded (`deno` vs `deno.worker`), and warns that `deno.*` libs
  are deprecated in favor of the types-based approach.
- Update docs, `deno init` templates, and the LSP to the new convention.
- Validate across real repos with the stock-ts-compat testbed before flipping any
  defaults.
- Open question: keep a deprecation-period shim (`deno.window` -> `deno`,
  `deno.worker` -> `deno.worker`) or hard-cut in Deno 3.

## Context

Split out of the native `deno check` work (#35938), which needs this for the
`deno.*`-lib spec tests (e.g. `tests/specs/check/tsconfig_files`,
`tsconfig_default_libs`). Those specs are being deferred in #35938 pending this
issue, since their correct output depends on the new model. This is an internal
convention change we intend to drive ourselves.

author: lenovouser

title: Deno desktop - subwindow close will close app and all other windows (macOS)
author: Blatman
